items.title: The Strange Ritual Before Medieval Duels: Why Opponents Touched
items.summary: Why did medieval opponents touch before combat? Lorris Chevalier explores the ritual, revealing how law and emotion intersected in duelling practices.

items.title: Indiana Jones aveva ragione? Un misterioso disco solare riemerge dalle montagne della Georgia: simbolo sacro, oggetto rituale o indizio di un culto dimenticato?
items.summary: Chi ha visto I predatori dell’arca perduta ricorda bene quel piccolo oggetto inciso, il “medaglione del sole”, capace di rivelare la posizione dell’Arca. Non era solo un espediente narrativo: l’idea del disco solare affonda le radici in simboli antichissimi, diffusi dall’Egitto…

items.title: La scoperta. Nel fondo di un vaso romano qualcosa è sopravvissuto per millenni: parassiti ancora riconoscibili. Che cosa mangiavano e bevevano davvero i Romani? E quali rischi correvano ogni giorno? “Il materiale trovato di Novae rappresenta la più antica evidenza finora conosciuta in Europa…”
items.summary: Nella quiete di una casa romana, lontano dai clamori dei fori e delle legioni, un gesto quotidiano si ripeteva ogni sera. Un oggetto semplice, quasi invisibile nella grande narrazione della storia: il vaso da notte. Eppure, proprio da questi contenitori umili, riemerge oggi una…

items.title: Camminano per i campi. Bip-bip. Un segnale. Scavano. Mio Dio, cosa sono! Sono di manifattura romana provinciale. A che servivano? Perchè uno dentro l’altro? C’è l’ombra di Asterix?
items.summary: Una bella passeggiata. Un po’ d’aria aperta, il fresco del mattino, la zona che sembra offrire spunti interessanti. I paesaggi potenzialmente archeologici si rivelano per una serie di dati. Esposizione al sole, vicinanza a corsi d’acqua, elementi in rilievo e bellezza. Ciò che…

items.title: Hélène Gestern, Sandro Veronesi, Emmanuelle de Boysson, Matisse, les femmes vikings… La sélection de livres de « Sud Ouest »

items.title: Archaeologists discover perfectly circular ancient Egyptian temple that may have been used for sacred water rituals
items.summary: The temple included a network of water infrastructure, revealing the importance of the ancient city and the Nile.

items.title: New Medieval Books: Vikings Behaving Reasonably
items.summary: This book offers more than just a clever title. It shows how Norse communities relied on the ideal of hóf—a cultural expectation of moderation and restraint—to resolve disputes and maintain order, often without the need for a strong central authority.

items.title: Les cryptes de l'église de Sant Rafèu
items.summary: Visite du site avec Anne Joncheray, directrice du Musée archéologique de Saint-Raphaël, Aurélie Dumont et Nathalie Molina, archéologues responsables d'opération de l'Inrap.

items.title: Un ragazzino di 13 anni passeggia in un campo e trova una moneta misteriosissima. Che ci fa Troia in Germania? Il reperto ha 2300 anni ed è totalmente “fuori luogo”
items.summary: All’inizio è solo un piccolo oggetto scuro che affiora dal terreno, senza alcun segno evidente del suo valore. Un ragazzo di tredici anni sta osservando il suolo di un campo agricolo nella periferia occidentale di Berlino, un paesaggio fatto di terra lavorata, silenzi e tracce…

items.title: Trovano una strana statua sotto il giardino. Gli archeologi: “Ha più di mille anni”. Identificata la divinità. Le sue proprietà magiche e preziose

items.title: How to find a billion-dollar shipwreck

items.title: Il sole in una scheggia. Scoperte “banche” neolitiche di cristallo di rocca. A cosa serviva? Cosa stanno trovando gli archeologi
items.summary: Una materia trasparente come l’acqua, luminosa come il sole, rara, selettiva, difficile da reperire. Un ghiaccio di pietra che dava forza e potere. Un elemento distintivo che poteva essere appannaggio dei vertici della società. Gli archeologi hanno scoperto centri neolitici…

items.title: Anglo-Saxon burial holds an older sister cradling her little brother after they both died 1,400 years ago, possibly of an infectious disease
items.summary: An ancient-DNA analysis of a rare Anglo-Saxon double burial revealed the people in the grave were brother and sister.

items.title: New Online Course: Exploring the Troubadours, Trobairitz, and Trouvères
items.summary: This three-week course begins on April 18th, with live sessions each Saturday from 1:00 to 3:00 pm EST.

items.title: La scoperta. Perchè non ricostruire? Perché a Pompei vigne e giardini, orti e frutteti presero il posto della case cadute nel terremoto del 62? Uno studio sulla rioccupazione del suolo dopo il sisma che precedette il disastro finale
items.summary: Non furono solo le ceneri del Vesuvio a cancellare la città. Prima della catastrofe del 79 d.C., Pompei aveva già attraversato una crisi profonda provocata dal grande terremoto del 62 d.C. Interi isolati lesionati, edifici collassati, infrastrutture compromesse. Eppure, proprio…

items.title: 'We all screamed when it happened': Bright-green fireball meteor caught exploding over famous Viking raid site in UK
items.summary: Photographers caught a spectacular emerald-green fireball meteor streaking above Lindisfarne in northeast England, where Viking raiders famously killed and robbed Christian monks in the eighth century.

items.title: Descubren por qué los propietarios decidieron plantar viñedos y huertos en lugar de reconstruir sus edificios tras el terremoto del 62 d.C. en Pompeya
items.summary: Pompeya no murió de repente. Antes de que el Vesubio la sepultara en el año 79 d.C., la ciudad ya había sobrevivido a otra catástrofe: un terremoto devastador en el año 62 que derribó edificios, agrietó calles y dejó barrios enteros en ruinas. Durante años, la arqueología ha…

items.title: Meravigliosa potenza. Scoperta ora nel terreno del forte romano di Vindolanda. Cos’è? Non è un amo, né un ancoretto, né un’ancora. Perchè così lunga? In che anni venne usata? Come funzionava?
items.summary: Impero romano e Roma – Tremano un po’ le mani, nel tenerla sul palmo. E’ ingombrante, possente. Immagini le notti nel forte e questo ferro, in saccoccia. I rumori delle porte. Immagini all’esterno, nel momento in cui la fortezza fu abbandonata. I suoni inquietanti di qualcuno…

items.title: Scoperta unica. Una città cristiana distrutta da un terremoto e un rito sorprendente. Gli archeologi trovano un blocco di marmo misterioso. A cosa serviva?
items.summary: All’inizio è solo una stanza che riemerge lentamente dalla terra. Le superfici si delineano, le murature si ricompongono, lo spazio sembra appartenere a una funzione già nota. Poi emergono dettagli che non coincidono con nessun modello conosciuto: una vasca collocata in…

items.title: Amarna (Part I)

items.title: The Libyan pharaohs of Egypt
items.summary: Aidan Dodson explores the history of the kings of Libyan ancestry who dominated Egypt during the 10th to 7th centuries BC.

items.title: Strange mammal ancestor laid huge, leathery eggs —‬ and it was key to surviving the world's worst mass extinction
items.summary: Using synchrotron X-ray CT scans of a fossilized, intact embryo, researchers found evidence that the plant-eating mammal Lystrosaurus laid eggs, which answers a key question about mammalian evolution.

items.summary: La construcción de un nuevo parque eólico en las inmediaciones de las pedanías alemanas de Ahlum y Dettum, un proyecto impulsado por la empresa SAB WindTeam GmbH que contempla la instalación de diecinueve aerogeneradores, ha destapado un capítulo insospechado de la prehistoria…

items.summary: A joint British-Austrian team has identified Egypt’s oldest known rotary tool. The small metal drill was found at Badari more than 100 years ago, and had attracted little attention until now. The

items.title: Du marais à la ville : autour du site des halles de Chambéry (Savoie)

items.title: Scientists just solved a 160-million-year fossil mystery “I’ve never seen anything like it”
items.summary: A rare fossil discovery is shedding light on the “missing years” of early sponge evolution. Scientists found a 550-million-year-old sponge that likely lacked hard skeletal parts, explaining why earlier fossils are so scarce. This supports the idea that the earliest sponges were…

items.title: Did People Run in the Middle Ages?
items.summary: Running in the Middle Ages was more common than expected, with medieval sources revealing evidence of speed, endurance, and foot travel among messengers, soldiers, and ordinary people.

items.title: The painted tomb of Insneferuishetef
items.summary: Wolfram Grajetzki explores rare painted scenes from an Old Kingdom mastaba at Dahshur.

items.summary: In the aftermath of Earth’s most catastrophic extinction event, one unlikely survivor rose to dominate a shattered world: Lystrosaurus. Now, a stunning fossil discovery—an ancient egg containing a curled-up embryo—has finally answered a decades-old mystery about whether mammal…

items.summary: Our series highlighting often overlooked Egyptologists continues with the legacy of a founder of the discipline, reassessed by Amandine Marshall.

items.title: Homo erectus' tools include stunning geodes and fossils, possibly as a way to connect with the cosmos, study finds
items.summary: Homo erectus may have deliberately selected rocks embedded with fossils and crystals to craft their hand axes — possibly to serve as mediators between humans and the cosmos.

items.title: Grèce antique : 19 monuments en danger
items.summary: En Grèce, 19 monuments hérités de l’Antiquité sont aujourd’hui menacés par des risques naturels croissants. Leur protection devient une priorité face à des dégradations accélérées.

items.title: Medieval Solar Storm Detected Through Tree Rings and Historical Records
items.summary: A medieval solar storm detected in tree rings reveals hidden radiation and challenges what historians thought about the famous auroras of 1204.

items.title: 110,000-year-old discovery rewrites human history: Neanderthals and Homo sapiens worked together
items.summary: The first-ever published research on Tinshemet Cave reveals that Neanderthals and Homo sapiens in the mid-Middle Paleolithic Levant not only coexisted but actively interacted, sharing technology, lifestyles, and burial customs. These interactions fostered cultural exchange,…

items.title: Cómo una jirafa con la que Lorenzo de Médici quiso emular a Julio César terminó salvando a la familia
items.summary: Es casi seguro que ningún lector sabrá qué animal es un camelopardo. En sentido estricto, ninguno; pero, si lo decimos en latín, se trata de la denominación científica de una especie: Giraffa camelopardalis, que así llamaron los romanos a la jirafa, al considerarla un híbrido de…

items.title: Native Americans were gambling with dice 6,000 years earlier than anyone else, study says
items.summary: Archaeological record suggests hunter gatherers were playing games of chance at the end of the last ice age Native American hunter gatherers were using dice for gaming and gambling more than 6,000 years before the practice appeared anywhere else, a new study argues. It says dice…

items.title: Les ancêtres des mammifères pondaient bien des œufs
items.summary: Les lointains ancêtres des mammifères, qui vivaient il y a 250 millions d'années, pondaient des oeufs, selon l'analyse d'un fossile d'herbivore découvert en Afrique du Sud.

items.title: Dans l'assiette des Européens, 10.000 ans d'inégalités de genre
items.summary: Des archéologues ont mis en évidence l'existence d'inégalités alimentaires fortement marquées par le genre depuis 10.000 ans en Europe, les femmes consommant systématiquement moins de protéines animales que les hommes.

items.title: Catacombes, musées, laboratoires : les restes humains, des vestiges pas comme les autres

items.title: Des restes d’Amérindiens kaliña guyanais dans les limbes du Musée de l’homme
items.summary: La loi française permet désormais la restitution à d’autres Etats de restes humains détenus dans des collections publiques. Mais ce n’est toujours pas le cas pour les territoires ultramarins.

items.title: Enfance : ce que l'archéologie raconte des premiers âges de la vie

items.summary: Découvert dans le sud de l'Espagne, un os carpien accrédite l'usage d'éléphants par le général carthaginois dans sa guerre contre Rome.

items.summary: Oxford Cotswold Archaeology’s ongoing excavations on the site of the planned Sizewell C power station in Suffolk have uncovered a vast array of features and finds spanning 6,000 years of history. Project Manager Richard Mortimer guides us through some of the most significant…

items.title: La plus vieille chélicère éclaire la trajectoire des araignées
items.summary: Un fossile vieux de 500 millions d’années met au jour la plus ancienne chélicère connue et repousse l’origine des chélicérates de 20 millions d’années. Une pièce clé pour comprendre l’émergence des ancêtres des araignées.

items.title: Antonio et Bruno, trésors fossiles de Trieste
items.summary: Près de Trieste, en Italie, le site de Villaggio del Pescatore a livré deux fossiles de dinosaures exceptionnels. Antonio est l’un des squelettes les plus complets au monde, tandis que Bruno, figé dans une faille sismique, présente une posture spectaculaire.

items.title: Who cares for places of worship?
items.summary: Under the rather ponderous headline ‘Revealing Misunderstandings about Stewardship of Our Ancient Churches’, Historic England has just published the results of its survey into public awareness of the crisis facing the nation’s places of worship. The results show that people are…

items.title: Un fossile égyptien bouscule les origines des grands singes
items.summary: Découverte en Égypte, une mandibule étend la présence des grands singes bien plus au nord qu’on ne le pensait.

items.title: Calendario de presentaciones abril-mayo
items.summary: En abril y mayo, nos multiplicamos con presentaciones, ferias, conferencias, firmas, mesas redondas... ¡Toma nota! La entrada Calendario de presentaciones abril-mayo aparece primero en Desperta Ferro Ediciones.

items.title: Scientists say we’ve been looking in the wrong place for human origins
items.summary: A fossil ape discovered in northern Egypt is reshaping the story of human evolution. The species, Masripithecus, lived about 17 to 18 million years ago and may sit very close to the ancestor of all modern apes. This finding challenges the long-standing focus on East Africa.…

items.title: El simbolismo de la muerte en la Semana Santa española
items.summary: Con la llegada de la Semana Santa, los mecanismos que mueven la estética macabra salen a la calle, activando unas pasiones que se tornan colectivas. La muerte ocupará un lugar central en estos días, pero su presencia puede ser analizada desde ópticas muy distintas. La entrada El…

items.title: Des vestiges de l’ancien Forum romain refont surface en plein cœur de Barcelone
items.summary: À Barcelone, un pavage monumental datant de la fondation de la colonie romaine de Barcino a été mis au jour sous une maison médiévale transformée en hôtel.

items.title: Skeleton found in Dutch church may be famous musketeer D'Artagnan – video
items.summary: Remains of the famed French musketeer Charles de Batz-Castelmore, known as d'Artagnan, may have been found in front of a church altar in the Dutch city of Maastricht, church officials and an archaeologist said. Workers discovered a grave containing human bones after parts of the…

items.title: Scientists found a rhino in the Arctic and it changes everything
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a new species of rhinoceros in the Canadian High Arctic, revealing that rhinos once lived far farther north than expected. The fossil, dating back 23 million years, is unusually complete and has helped reshape ideas about how these animals migrated…

items.title: Bataille épique dans les mers du Crétacé
items.summary: Dans les mers du Crétacé nord-américain, les grands prédateurs ne se contentaient pas de chasser de petites proies. Un fossile d'Alabama montre qu'un immense poisson osseux a mordu un reptile marin de quatre mètres.

items.title: This crocodile ran like a greyhound across prehistoric Britain 200 million years ago
items.summary: A newly discovered Triassic reptile from the UK looked more like a racing greyhound than a crocodile, built for speed on land. With long legs and a lightweight body, it hunted small animals in a dry, upland environment millions of years ago. Scientists identified it as a new…

items.title: Archaeological site in Chile upends theory of how humans populated the Americas … again
items.summary: Discovery at Monte Verde puts north-to-south expansion theory back at centre of heated debate on continent’s human history A groundbreaking new study may have once again upended our understanding of human prehistory in the Americas. For years, the predominant theory of how…

items.title: De nouveaux « Gaulois assis » découverts à Dijon
items.summary: Cinq sépultures énigmatiques datant de 400 à 200 avant J.-C. font l’objet de fouilles archéologiques, après treize tombes du même type dégagées en 2025, et deux en 1992.

items.title: Ancient skeleton unearthed in France is latest to be found sitting upright
items.summary: Scientists trying to work out why Gauls chose to bury some of their dead in seated position facing west Children at a primary school in eastern France found a strange attraction next to their playground this week: a skeleton sitting upright, peeking out of a circular pit. It is…

items.title: These dinosaurs had wings but couldn’t fly
items.summary: Some feathered dinosaurs may have briefly taken to the skies—only to give it up later. By studying rare fossils with preserved feathers, researchers uncovered a surprising clue hidden in molting patterns, revealing that Anchiornis likely couldn’t fly at all. Instead of the neat,…

items.title: Roma – Crypta Balbi. Il tempo verticale
items.summary: Archeologia Viva n. 236 – marzo/aprile 2026 pp. 4-5 La Crypta Balbi, oggi una delle sedi del Museo Nazionale Romano e in origine parte di un complesso teatrale fatto costruire da Lucio Cornelio Balbo nel 13 a.C. in Campo Marzio, conserva al suo interno tracce di edifici e…

items.title: Minoici e Micenei. Omaggio al Grande Re
items.summary: Archeologia Viva n. 236 – marzo/aprile 2026 pp. 20-32 di Louis Godart Una nuova lettura delle testimonianze archeologiche ed epigrafiche minoiche e micenee – abbinata a un confronto con il racconto omerico – ha portato l’autore dell’articolo (fra i maggiori esperti di civiltà…

items.title: Luce da Oriente. Scavi italiani nel Sultanato dell’Oman
items.summary: Archeologia Viva n. 236 – marzo/aprile 2026 pp. 48-58 di Marco Ramazzotti La lettura di un paesaggio archeologico plurimillenario consente di ricostruire la vicenda di una popolazione stanziata nel sud-est della Penisola Arabica che fra le età del Bronzo e del Ferro tenne testa…

items.title: The representation of isolated legs in Middle Magdalenian portable art: a widespread and shared symbolic motif
items.summary: El Magdaleniense Medio (14 500-13 000 BP) se caracteriza por producciones simbólicas y artísticas compartidas a gran distancia. En este artículo se analiza una de esas producciones escasamente atendida en la investigación: las representaciones aisladas de patas de herbívoros…

items.title: Estudio de un depósito ritual de las guerras astur-cántabras: el conjunto del puñal de filos curvos de la cueva de La Cerrosa-Lagaña (Suarías, Asturias, España) como vínculo entre los cinturones de los puñales indígenas y el cingulum romano
items.summary: Durante las cinco campañas de excavación (2020-2023 y 2025) en la cueva de La Cerrosa-Lagaña (Suarías, Peñamellera Baja, Asturias, España) se han ido recuperando piezas pertenecientes a una vaina de puñal de filos curvos y su cinturón articulado de suspensión. En este estudio se…

items.title: El castro de Elviña (A Coruña, Galicia): monumentalización, espacios rituales y contactos en el final de la Edad del Hierro
items.summary: El yacimiento de Elviña (A Coruña) es uno de los castros más conocidos del noroeste peninsular, tanto por la precocidad de las intervenciones arqueológicas desarrolladas en él, desde los años cuarenta del pasado siglo, como por los hallazgos singulares de piezas de orfebrería.…

items.title: En torno a las raíces locales de la romanización. Una propuesta desde el territorium Dianensis
items.summary: Este artículo examina las transformaciones del paisaje humano en el sector oriental de la península ibérica durante la transición entre el mundo ibérico y el romano. El estudio se centra específicamente en las comarcas centro-meridionales valencianas que delimitan el antiguo…

items.title: Proposta d’un indice per l’impiego economico nell’architettura romana: applicazione al caso dei templi del culto imperiale in Hispania
items.summary: Questo studio propone un approccio innovativo per l’analisi dei costi di costruzione in età romana, introducendo l’Indice d’Impegno Economico come parametro comparativo. L’obiettivo è ridurre l’incidenza delle incertezze dimensionali, planimetriche e logistiche introducendo un…

items.title: Los grafitos parietales latinos y griegos de Hispania (ss. I-V d.C.)
items.summary: El objetivo de este trabajo es ofrecer un estado de la cuestión sobre los grafitos parietales de época imperial procedentes de la Hispania romana. Se recopilan aquellos grafitos de carácter epigráfico (palabras aisladas, monolíteros, numerales y textos más complejos) escritos…

items.title: Isabel Rondán-Sevilla (2025) Investigación histórica no invasiva: ensayo de aplicación en asentamientos romanos en la Hispania Meridional. Cádiz: Editorial Universidad de Cádiz. ISBN 9788498289602. 181 pp.
items.summary: Reseña de la obra: Investigación histórica no invasiva: ensayo de aplicación en asentamientos romanos en la Hispania Meridional

items.title: 400 million-year-old fish fossils reveal how life began moving onto land
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered new clues about some of Earth’s earliest fish, shedding light on the ancient origins of vertebrates that eventually moved onto land. By reanalyzing mysterious fossils from Australia’s famed Gogo Formation and studying a newly reconstructed…

items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized…

items.title: « Ici on pratique l’archéologie comme au siècle dernier » : le patrimoine ivoirien encore largement sous-exploité
items.summary: En Côte d’Ivoire, l’archéologie est l’une des disciplines les plus pauvrement dotées : les chercheurs n’ont pas accès aux techniques de télédétection pour la prospection des sites et les artefacts doivent partir en France faute de laboratoire de datation au carbone.

items.title: Which human remains are held in UK museums – and where?
items.summary: Institutions including the Natural History Museum and Cambridge University hold more than 263,000 human remains, Guardian investigation finds Vast scale of overseas human remains held in UK museums decried by MPs and experts Descendants of Zimbabwe resistance heroes urge UK to…

items.title: La muerte de Urraca I de León y Castilla, nueve siglos después
items.summary: El 8 de marzo de 1126, hace hoy 900 años, fallecía en el castillo de Saldaña la primera reina titular y soberana por derecho propio de la península ibérica y del Occidente medieval: Urraca I de León y Castilla. La historiadora Sonia Vital, autora del libro Urraca. Una reina en…

items.title: Un singular depósito de lingotes de cobre en La Celada (Tafalla, Navarra)
items.summary: Un excepcional hallazgo en el yacimiento de La Celada ha sacado a la luz un singular depósito de lingotes de cobre del Bronce Medio, único en la península ibérica. Más allá de su carácter inédito, el conjunto ofrece nuevas claves sobre cómo se producía, almacenaba y redistribuía…

items.title: 2700-year-old teeth reveal the hidden lives of Iron Age Italians
items.summary: Iron Age teeth from southern Italy have become time capsules, preserving intimate details of childhood and diet. Growth lines in the enamel reveal moments of early-life stress, while hardened plaque holds microscopic remains of cereals, legumes, and fermented foods. The findings…

items.title: ‘The Donald Trump of ancient Egypt’: Ramses II’s ego is on full display in new exhibition
items.summary: A collection of 3,000-year-old artefacts at Battersea power station gives Egypt’s most ambitious, self-aggrandising pharaoh a chance to emerge from Tutankhamun’s shadow The mummy of Egypt’s most ambitious pharaoh, Ramses II (often spelt Ramesses), is a masterpiece of the…

items.title: T rex breath and Queen Elizabeth’s car: scientists creating ‘time machine for the nose’
items.summary: Researchers are recreating ancient odours for museumgoers as interest in the archaeology of smell grows From the interior of Queen Elizabeth II’s car to the scent of ancient Egyptian funerary practices, museumgoers are getting a whiff of the past like never before. Experts say…

items.title: 40,000-year-old signs show humans were recording information long before writing
items.summary: More than 40,000 years ago, Ice Age humans were carving repeated patterns of dots, lines, and crosses into tools and small ivory figurines. A new computational study of more than 3,000 of these Paleolithic signs reveals that they were not random decorations but structured…

items.title: Urraca I de León y Castilla ¿la primera reina de Europa?
items.summary: La afirmación de que Urraca fue «la primera reina de Europa» se ha repetido con frecuencia, con mayor o menor acierto, por todo tipo de autores que en los últimos años se han acercado a su figura para escribir sobre ella. Sin duda, se trata de un enunciado potente y atractivo,…

items.title: Black Gold: Sumerian Bitumen Recipes Worked Like Asphalt, 4,000 Years Ago
items.summary: Black Gold: Sumerian Bitumen Recipes Worked Like Asphalt, 4,000 Years Ago Sumerian craftspeople in Mesopotamia didn’t simply scoop up natural bitumen and slap it on boats or bricks. A new materials study suggests they followed repeatable “recipes” that controlled strength,…

items.title: La règle d’un jeu romain débloquée par l’IA
items.summary: Des chercheurs ont eu recours à des agents d’intelligence artificielle pour percer le secret d’un plateau en calcaire présenté au Musée Het Romeins à Heerlen (Pays-Bas).

items.title: Alleged cat burglar arrested after priceless Egyptian artefacts taken in Queensland museum heist
items.summary: Man charged after 2,600-year-old cat sculpture, mummy mask and necklace stolen from Caboolture museum Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Queensland police have arrested a man accused of staging a brazen cat burglary of priceless Egyptian artefacts from a…

items.title: En Normandie, la découverte d’une tombe de l’âge du bronze éclaire sur les structures sociales de l’époque
items.summary: Une équipe d’archéologues a mis au jour une sépulture de l’âge du bronze ancien contenant des pointes de flèche en silex et des poignards, des objets considérés comme des symboles de pouvoir.

items.title: « Un programme renforcé pour la surveillance du patrimoine archéologique en Ukraine est nécessaire »
items.summary: De victime collatérale à cible délibérée, le patrimoine est au cœur de la guerre totale pour le contrôle des plaines ukrainiennes, observe, dans une tribune au « Monde », Henri de Mégille, de la délégation au patrimoine de l’armée de terre.

items.title: I ragazzi, lo smartphone (e noi adulti): il nuovo libro di Carlo Verdelli
items.summary: Esce il 5 febbraio per Einaudi Stile libero «Il diavolo in tasca. Genitori e figli prigionieri del telefonino». Le presentazioni a Milano e Roma

items.title: From Dorset to the world: wave of donations helps to secure Cerne giant’s home
items.summary: Support from more than 20 countries propels National Trust to its target to protect chalk figure and local wildlife It feels like a very British monument: a huge chalk figure carved into a steep Dorset hillside that for centuries has intrigued lovers of English folklore and…

items.title: Mass grave in Jordan sheds new light on world’s earliest recorded pandemic
items.summary: Researchers tell ‘human story’ about crisis during plague of Justinian, which killed millions in Byzantine empire A US-led research team has verified the first Mediterranean mass grave of the world’s earliest recorded pandemic, providing stark new details about the plague of…

items.title: Le trésor de Lavau se dévoile au Musée d’art moderne de Troyes, onze ans après les fouilles
items.summary: Bijoux d’or, vaisselle de banquet et chaudron géant : le trésor de Lavau révèle les fastes et la diversité culturelle d’une élite celte méconnue, à découvrir pour la première fois au Musée d’art moderne de Troyes.

items.title: Country diary: Mammoth and bear, replaced by mattress and beer can | Michael J Warren
items.summary: Grays, Essex: I visit an unassuming gorge where a wealth of ice age fossils was once found, telling us about Britain’s megafauna – and Neanderthals You wouldn’t know the Lion Pit was there. This overgrown gorge exists quietly, without the sensation its name implies, below a…
